comment.body:
take trading as collecting points, (including fees and taxes) - just as Wyckoff said.

i already gave you practical ideas to calculate your money used for trading, sizing, etc.

you mark pivots, 
watch the order flow, volume profile,
jump in when dynamics favor your bias (like you go long, when you see constant increasing buying volume and decreasing selling volume when spot is approaching your target support level),
mark stop-loss when you leave the trade

thats it.
comment.raw_body:
take trading as collecting points, (including fees and taxes) - just as Wyckoff said.

i already gave you practical ideas to calculate your money used for trading, sizing, etc.

you mark pivots, 
watch the order flow, volume profile,
jump in when dynamics favor your bias (like you go long, when you see constant increasing buying volume and decreasing selling volume when spot is approaching your target support level),
mark stop-loss when you leave the trade

thats it.

comment.body:
so bcs if the market already agreed on a price level lets say higher,
but all of a sudden, when market rebound to that pivotal level, you see that buying volume is not increasing and bears take control, that means that something has changed.
when market retests it, you will see, how the bull interest changed: got higher or lower...
but you will see if something changed in terms of the market's relation to that level
